description: Subclass fails to provide the 'axes' attribute required by base class methods
reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because ClusterGrid inherits from Grid (axisgrid.py), but its __init__ method fails to define the 'axes' attribute. The base class method Grid.add_legend (axisgrid.py, line 150) explicitly relies on self.axes.flat[0]. Consequently, ClusterGrid is not substitutable for Grid, as calling add_legend on a ClusterGrid instance will result in an AttributeError.

description: Monkey-patching the 'draw' method of a base type instance alters its behavioral contract
reasoning: Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because this method replaces the 'draw' method of a matplotlib.collections.PathCollection instance with a custom local function. This violates the behavioral contract of the matplotlib Artist hierarchy; any consumer expecting a standard PathCollection will now trigger Seaborn-specific beeswarm logic during the rendering pass, which is an unexpected side effect for that type.

description: Type-checking (isinstance) used to branch logic instead of polymorphic dispatch
reasoning: Switch Statements on type smell -> LSP violation because the method uses isinstance(ax_obj, mpl.axes.Axes) to determine how to add a legend. This breaks polymorphism; if a new subtype of a grid or axes is introduced that doesn't inherit from the specific classes checked, the logic fails or defaults to an incorrect assumption (the 'else' block assuming FacetGrid API).

description: Subclass overrides class attributes used for data mapping, changing base class contract
reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because _CategoricalAggPlotter overrides the 'flat_structure' attribute defined in _CategoricalPlotter. This attribute is used by the base class VectorPlotter to define how data is assigned to variables. By changing this mapping from {'y': '@values'} to {'x': '@index', 'y': '@values'}, the subclass alters the fundamental data-handling contract of the base type.

description: Subclass mutates base class state and structure in a way that breaks mapping invariants
reasoning: Inappropriate Intimacy smell -> LSP violation because the subclass constructor mutates the 'plot_data' and 'variables' state established by the base class VectorPlotter. Specifically, it renames columns (line 80) and injects dummy data (line 104) to force categorical rules. This breaks the post-conditions of the base class initialization, as the resulting object no longer adheres to the standard variable mapping contract expected by other VectorPlotter methods.
